claim: 63% of all stray dogs received by Dallas Animal Services came from just 7 zip codes, enabling geographically targeted reunification programs.
stance_target: geographic concentration of strays as basis for targeted outreach and search radius prioritization
scope_conditions: Dallas Animal Services, fiscal year 2019; Kremer 2021; n=30,609 dogs
quoted_support: 63% of the stray dogs DAS took in came from seven zip codes. Seventy percent of the stray dogs were found less than one mile from their homes. Forty-two percent were less than 400 feet from their homes.

claim: Over 90% of dogs who were returned to their owners completed the reunion in less than 5 days — DAS's hold period revision was data-confirmed.
stance_target: 5-day temporal window as the critical intervention threshold; search effort should be maximum-intensity in first 5 days
scope_conditions: Dallas Animal Services, fiscal year 2019; Kremer 2021 Shelter RTOs
quoted_support: Over 90% of the dogs who got home to their families, did so in less than five days.
